title: linaria origanifolia subsp. bourgaei
intro: Linaria origanifolia subsp. bourgaei, also known as Bourgae's Toadflax, is a plant species in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in the healing of wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial properties. Linaria origanifolia subsp. bourgaei can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
